data.text: Issue directive 0 by doing the required launch disposition assessment first: inspect the priority and cite a checkable signal. If nothing actionable remains, wrap as archive-candidate or awaiting-founder with zero delegated build atoms; if concrete in-priority work exists, delegate the first real build atom. Do not create a no-op or reaffirmation build atom.
data.rationale: Deb status has remained at atom 0 awaiting Oscar's first directive since 2026-06-23 07:44:33 MDT with lastDirectiveAt null, Bob standby, verify idle, and no faults. The run objective specifically requires assessment before any build and no fake-build loop.

data.task:
Atom 1 of the `launch-disposition-first` priority — give the runner a mechanical, recorded **wrap disposition** so an archive-ready launch is distinguishable from an awaiting-founder one, and so a fake-build can never be reported as archive-ready.

CONTEXT: Today the runner has no first-class launch-disposition concept. `deriveWrapupRunStatus` (packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts ~line 305) collapses every "Archive ready" closeout AND every founder-decision-needed closeout into the same RunStatus `awaiting-founder`. The priority's vocabulary (archive-candidate / awaiting-founder / actionable) exists only in prose. This atom adds the mechanical primitive the proof harness (a LATER atom — do NOT build it now) will assert on.

WHAT TO CHANGE (packages/core only):
1. Add a derivation `deriveWrapDisposition(markdown, contract, builderDispatchCount)` near `deriveWrapupRunStatus` in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts. Type it as a local exported `WrapDisposition = 'archive-candidate' | 'awaiting-founder' | 'continue'`. Rules, in order:
   - if `founderDecisionNeeded(...)` is true -> 'awaiting-founder';
   - else if the closeout's runStatus section matches the existing `/^\s*archive ready\b/i` test AND `builderDispatchCount === 0` -> 'archive-candidate';
   - else -> 'continue'.
   The `builderDispatchCount === 0` guard is the no-fake-build invariant: a run that delegated even one build atom can NEVER resolve to archive-candidate — it falls through to 'continue' (or 'awaiting-founder' if a decision is needed). Reuse the existing `founderDecisionNeeded`, `founderCloseoutSection`, `section` helpers; derive everything from the validated closeout markdown — do NOT introduce a second copy of the closeout contract.
2. At the wrap site (where `deriveWrapupRunStatus` is already called on the validated wrapup closeout), count the run's `builder-dispatch` events from the store for this run, call `deriveWrapDisposition`, and record ONE new event `store.recordEvent({ runId, type: 'wrap-disposition', data: { disposition, buildAtoms } })`. Keep the existing `deriveWrapupRunStatus`/RunStatus behavior exactly as-is — do NOT add a value to the `RunStatus` union and do NOT touch the RunStatus switch sites (archive-candidate runs keep setting run status `awaiting-founder`; the new signal is the recorded disposition event, not a RunStatus change). This keeps blast radius contained.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (add unit tests to packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts):
  (a) a wrapup delivered as the FIRST directive (zero `builder-dispatch` events) whose closeout runStatus is "Archive ready" and needs no founder decision -> a `wrap-disposition` event with `disposition: 'archive-candidate'`, `buildAtoms: 0`;
  (b) a wrapup closeout that DOES need a founder decision -> `disposition: 'awaiting-founder'` (regardless of "Archive ready");
  (c) a run that delegated >=1 atom (>=1 `builder-dispatch`) and then wraps with an "Archive ready" closeout -> disposition is NOT 'archive-candidate' (it is 'continue' or 'awaiting-founder') — proving no-fake-build.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing `deriveWrapupRunStatus` -> awaiting-founder behavior and all existing runner.test.ts cases; RunStatus union stays 5 values; typecheck stays clean.

WRITE-SCOPE (do not touch anything else): packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts. (Only add a type/helper inside runner.ts; no store schema or RunStatus changes.)

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, run both and paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec vitest run tests/runner.test.ts` is green AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` (or the repo's core typecheck script) is clean. Do NOT build the proof harness, Oz surfaces, or docs in this atom.

data.task:
Atom 2 of `launch-disposition-first` — make `archive-candidate` require a CITED CHECKABLE SIGNAL, so 'archive-ready' means tested, not merely asserted (Objective proof obligation (c)). Builds directly on atom 1's `deriveWrapDisposition` in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts.

CONTEXT: Today `deriveWrapDisposition` returns 'archive-candidate' for any 'Archive ready' closeout with zero build atoms and no founder decision — even if the closeout only asserts completeness in prose. The Objective requires the archive-ready verdict to be backed by a checkable signal (e.g. a one-command proof harness / test command), not a bare narrative claim. Do NOT add a new wrap-up Play section or a second contract — derive the signal from the EXISTING validated closeout markdown the runner already has.

WHAT TO CHANGE (packages/core only):
1. Add a helper `closeoutCitesCheckableSignal(markdown: string): string | null` near `deriveWrapDisposition`. It returns the first matched runnable-verification reference found anywhere in the closeout markdown, or null if none. A checkable signal = a runnable command/test reference. Match (case-insensitive) any of: a fenced ``` code block; `node scripts/<...>.mjs` or a bare `scripts/<...>.mjs` path; a `pnpm ...` command that runs tests; `vitest`; a `cocoder ...` CLI invocation; or a `*.test.*` / `*.spec.*` path token. Return the matched substring (trimmed, reasonably bounded) so it can be recorded. Keep the matcher small and readable; do not over-engineer the regex.
2. Tighten `deriveWrapDisposition`: the 'archive-candidate' branch must ALSO require `closeoutCitesCheckableSignal(markdown) !== null`. When the closeout is 'Archive ready' + zero build atoms + no founder decision but NO checkable signal is cited, it must fall through to 'continue' (the no-bare-assertion guard) — never 'archive-candidate'. Order: founder decision -> 'awaiting-founder'; else archive-ready + zero atoms + cited signal -> 'archive-candidate'; else -> 'continue'.
3. At the wrap recording site (where the `wrap-disposition` event is recorded), include the matched signal: change the event data to `{ disposition, buildAtoms, signal }` where `signal` is `closeoutCitesCheckableSignal(pickup)` (string or null). Keep the existing `deriveWrapupRunStatus`/RunStatus behavior unchanged.
4. Update the EXISTING atom-1 test 'archive-ready first-directive wrap records archive-candidate disposition with zero build atoms': its closeout currently cites no runnable command, so under the new guard it would (correctly) downgrade. Make that test's archive-ready closeout cite a checkable signal (e.g. include `node scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs` in the Judgment or What Changed text) so it still demonstrates 'archive-candidate', and assert the recorded event now includes a non-null `signal`. Leave the other two atom-1 tests intact (their expectations of 'awaiting-founder' and 'continue' still hold; update their expected event data to include `signal` as appropriate — null for the no-signal cases).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (add NEW tests to packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts):
  (c1) 'Archive ready' + zero build atoms + closeout cites a runnable command (e.g. `node scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs`) -> `wrap-disposition` event `{ disposition: 'archive-candidate', buildAtoms: 0, signal: <non-null matched string> }`;
  (c2) 'Archive ready' + zero build atoms + NO runnable command anywhere (bare narrative completeness only) -> disposition is 'continue' (NOT 'archive-candidate'), `signal: null`.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing `deriveWrapupRunStatus` -> awaiting-founder behavior; all other runner.test.ts cases; RunStatus union stays 5 values; typecheck clean.

WRITE-SCOPE (touch nothing else): packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, paste output of both): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec vitest run tests/runner.test.ts` green AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` clean. Do NOT build the proof harness, Oz surfaces, or docs in this atom.

data.task:
Atom 3 of `launch-disposition-first` — author the one-command proof harness `scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs` that the Objective's 'Verified by' requires. It must be a self-contained red/green proof of all three obligations, modeled CLOSELY on the existing `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` (read it first for structure, exec/vitest helpers, JSON reporter parsing, and the mutate -> rerun -> restore pattern).

WHAT THE HARNESS MUST PROVE (the disposition mechanism lives in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts: `deriveWrapDisposition` + `closeoutCitesCheckableSignal`, recorded as the `wrap-disposition` event; the unit tests are in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts):

PHASE 1 — GREEN BASELINE. Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec vitest run tests/runner.test.ts --reporter=json --outputFile=<tmp>` on the clean tree and assert it is fully green. This file already contains the three proof obligations: (a) an 'Archive ready' first-directive wrap with zero build atoms + a cited signal records `disposition: 'archive-candidate'` (test name contains 'archive-candidate disposition with zero build atoms'); (b) the multi-atom loop delegates the first atom for a `delegate` directive (an actionable priority still builds) — assert at least one existing test proving a delegate directive produces a `builder-dispatch` is present and green; (c) a bare 'Archive ready' wrap with no runnable signal records `continue` ('without a runnable signal records continue disposition'). Print each obligation as a PASS line.

PHASE 2 — THE NO-FAKE-BUILD GUARD IS LOAD-BEARING. Temporarily edit runner.ts to delete the `&& builderDispatchCount === 0` clause from `deriveWrapDisposition`, rerun ONLY the test named 'archive-ready wrap after a builder dispatch records continue disposition' (vitest `-t '<name>'`), and assert it now FAILS (because a fake-build would wrongly become archive-candidate). Then restore runner.ts byte-for-byte and assert that same test is green again.

PHASE 3 — THE CHECKABLE-SIGNAL GUARD IS LOAD-BEARING. Temporarily edit runner.ts to delete the `&& closeoutCitesCheckableSignal(markdown)` clause, rerun ONLY 'archive-ready first-directive wrap without a runnable signal records continue disposition', assert it now FAILS (a bare assertion would wrongly become archive-candidate). Restore byte-for-byte and assert green again.

HARD REQUIREMENTS:
- Read runner.ts into memory at start; perform every mutation as an in-memory string replace written to disk; ALWAYS restore the original bytes in a `finally` block so a thrown assertion never leaves the tree dirty. At the end, verify the file is byte-identical to the original (and ideally assert `git diff --quiet -- packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`).
- If a mutation's target substring is not found (code drifted), FAIL loudly with the expected substring — do not silently skip a phase (no silent caps).
- Print a clear final ledger and `process.exit(1)` if ANY check is not as expected; exit 0 only when all phases pass. Include a top-of-file usage comment: `node scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs`.
- Use only Node built-ins (node:child_process, node:fs/promises, node:path, node:url), same imports/style as proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs. Resolve repoRoot from import.meta.url.

WRITE-SCOPE (only this): scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs (new file). Do not edit runner.ts or tests except the transient in-harness mutations that you restore.

EXIT CRITERION (paste full output): `node scripts/proof-launch-disposition.mjs` prints all PASS lines and exits 0, AND `git status --porcelain` shows no residual change to packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts after it runs (tree left clean).
